label: Squarish 3BR / baths CLUSTERED with mid-band HALL — 3BR extension of the 2BR side_split_cl_hall: master at front of the private column, ensuite+common wet core and hall in the middle band, br3 and br2 paired across the rear; kitchen migrates to the rear of the public column. Open-plan great_room + kitchen.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — interior door from master, west side of the bath block
shared plumbing wall — both wet stacks in one bay, single vent run
master door opens north into the hall
br2 door opens south into the hall
br3 door opens south into the hall — the hall runs east-west along the rear pair's front walls so both standards reach it
common bath door opens east into the hall
hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style); kitchen sits at the rear of the public column

soft_proximities.note: keep the master-adjacent side of the wet core close to master
let br2 borrow the kitchen party wall so the rear band packs without a dead pocket

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr_3br
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Same tiering as the 2BR parent but triggered at 110 m². The rear pair is the pressure point: master is capped at 15 m² so br2/br3 hold >= 2.8 m clear width each. When preferred relaxes, kitchen never drops below 1.8 m; baths may fall toward the 1.2 m² hard min.

notes: 3BR squarish catalog topology #2 — SIDE_SPLIT with clustered wet core.
Direct 3BR extension of 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_cl_hall_gr. Natural floor
band ~92-106 m²; minimum feasible shell ~9.3 x 9.8 m.
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):
Front: [master               | great_room (entry) ]
Mid:   [ensuite|common| HALL | great_room         ]
Rear:  [br3      | br2      | kitchen             ]
Program: identical clustered bath block (ensuite + common share a wet_core)
and front-master position as the 2BR parent. The changes: (1) the rear of the
private column now holds a PAIR of standard bedrooms instead of one, so the
private column widens to ~6.0-6.4 m (2 x 3.0-3.2 m bedroom widths); (2) the
hall stretches east-west along the pair's front walls so BOTH standards door
into it; (3) the kitchen migrates from the public column's midpoint to its
rear, keeping the gr open plan and putting the dirty kitchen directly behind.
Ventilation: master fronts the street, br3/br2 hold the rear wall, great_room
holds front + side — no landlocked habitable room.
Privacy: best-in-class of the four 3BR squarish topologies — every bedroom
door faces the hall, no bedroom shares a wall with the great_room except
via the bath block buffer.
Fit warning: needs the widest shell of the family. If frontage < ~9.3 m the
rear pair collapses below 2.8 m widths — fall back to hall_core (#1) or
front_back_split (#3). When brief.carport_side='left', mirror the topology.
